mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signal expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in less than 55% of months with rising PAYEMS, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5% constitutes restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and slows corporate investment, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage at 5.24% as of Dec 2006
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60d returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and a stable or declining unemployment rate confirm a healthy consumer-driven expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rising with UNRATE flat or falling
failure_condition: Joint positive RSAFS and flat or falling UNRATE months fail to produce positive 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with accelerating INDPRO in late-cycle periods produce negative 5d returns less than 45% of the time, or mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede weaker consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20d returns in less than 45% of cases, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate and rising consumer price index indicates restrictive monetary policy confronting sticky inflation, compressing equity valuations through higher real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint rising FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL periods fail to produce negative 60d returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term does not improve prediction over single-series models (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million units signal strong residential investment and construction sector momentum that boosts cyclical earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances with HOUST above 1500 fail to produce positive 5d returns in more than 55% of cases, or mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth reflects broad-based aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue and profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive GDPC1 growth fail to produce positive 60d returns in more than 55% of cases, or rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
